page: Microsoft Azure E4-2as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The configuration includes 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The instance has no local storage and zero GPUs.

Benchmark results reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25 percent, while single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels. However, multi-core performance is weak, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25 percent and bottom 10 percent respectively. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only small block cached bandwidth rated as poor and uncached large-block read and write bandwidth rated as weak.

Given the high memory density relative to compute resources, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. It is suitable for small-scale database operations, caching layers, or text processing tasks that fit entirely within its 32.0 GB RAM. However, poor performance in multi-core benchmarks, software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations indicates it is not suited for compute-heavy parallel processing or high-throughput database workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E4-2as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 2 vCPUs from 1 physical core and 32.0 GB of RAM, offering a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB. It contains no local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, but weak multi-core execution and poor memory bandwidth. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute tasks. It is best suited for workloads such as memory-caching, text processing, and lightweight database operations that require a larger memory footprint without demanding high parallel processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4-2as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring a 3.525 GHz AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 2 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads with low parallel compute requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4-2as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, suited for low-compute, memory-heavy tasks.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and strong single-core performance for low-compute, memory-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
32.0 GB of RAM paired with 2 vCPUs
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for memory-caching and lightweight database tasks
